# Microcosm Organ Atlas

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_organ_atlas.py from core/organ_registry.json, core/organ_families.json, core/organ_atlas.json, core/organ_evidence_classes.json, core/substrate_substitution_ledger.json, core/paper_module_capsules.json, and core/architecture_kernel.json. Do not hand-edit; run: PYTHONPATH=src python3 scripts/build_organ_atlas.py --write -->

An **organ** is one accepted public runtime surface: a runnable specimen with a registry row, a claim ceiling, and receipts. This atlas is the comprehension layer over all 82 of them — what each one makes visible, what an agent runs it for, and what it does **not** authorize.

Most organs are **standalone specimens**: they do not call each other. They bind to one shared spine — the architecture kernel (`project -> catalog -> pattern -> standard -> route -> work -> event -> evidence -> explanation -> assimilation`), the evidence-class registry, and the authority ceiling. A few are **wired** to other named surfaces; each card says which. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) for the system shape and [README.md](README.md) for the first run.

> Family labels and counts are navigation metadata, not maturity, completeness, or release signals. Evidence classes are accounting for what each organ proves, not scores. Nothing here authorizes release, hosted deployment, provider calls, source mutation, private-root equivalence, or proof authority above each organ's own claim ceiling.

## Microcosm at a glance — every organ in one line

This is the lowest faithful pass over the public substrate: one row per accepted organ, grouped in canonical family order with Entry & Reveal first. One-lines and authority ceilings come from `core/paper_module_capsules.json::paper_modules[*].compression`; organ identity and commands come from the accepted registry/atlas model.

### Entry & Reveal at a glance

- **Cold Reader Route Map** (`cold_reader_route_map`) — The public cold-reader route-map fixture validates first-run command order, docs refs, receipt refs, authority ceilings, copied cold-entry source-module digests, and private-state exclusion without becoming route-registry authority. Evidence `semantic_validator`; first command `microcosm cold-reader-route-map run-route-map-bundle --input examples/cold_reader_route_map/exported_cold_reader_route_map_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/cold_reader_route_map --card`; claim ceiling: Public route-map fixture, exported-bundle receipts, and copied non-secret macro cold-entry body evidence only; no route-registry authority, provider call, source mutation, release approval, private-data equivalence, trading or financial advice, or whole-system correctness.; drilldown: [full card](#cold-reader-route-map) · [paper module](paper_modules/cold_reader_route_map.md); provenance `paper_module.cold_reader_route_map` (`direct`).
- **Public Reveal Walkthrough** (`public_reveal_walkthrough`) — The public reveal walkthrough validates a ten-minute cold-reader path through commands, routes, evidence refs, source-open body imports, negative cases, and authority ceilings without claiming release readiness or private-root equivalence. Evidence `bounded_runtime_computation`; first command `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.public_reveal_walkthrough run --input fixtures/first_wave/public_reveal_walkthrough/input --out receipts/first_wave/public_reveal_walkthrough`; claim ceiling: Public reveal fixture and exported-bundle receipts only; no release, hosted deployment, publication, recipient work, provider call, secret export, private-root equivalence, Lean/Lake execution, source mutation, or whole-system correctness.; drilldown: [full card](#public-reveal-walkthrough) · [paper module](paper_modules/public_reveal_walkthrough.md); provenance `paper_module.public_reveal_walkthrough` (`direct`).

### Why the counts are honest

`82` accepted organs · `97` JSON paper-module capsules · `100` Markdown paper modules · `99` JSON paper-module projections. These counts are not expected to match: `15` capsules are mechanism-only, `2` mechanism-only capsules are used by accepted organs through explicit paper-module references, and `15` capsule rows are not accepted-organ atlas rows. Accepted organs, JSON paper-module capsules, and Markdown/JSON paper module projections are distinct surfaces. A count mismatch is expected when capsules describe mechanism-only or ahead-of-registry rows, or when Markdown/JSON projections exist outside the accepted-organ registry.

### First command

Run `microcosm tour --card .` for the local first-screen path, then use [AGENT_ROUTES.md](AGENT_ROUTES.md) for task-class routing or the family sections below for per-organ cards.

### What this does not authorize

Family labels and counts are navigation metadata, not maturity, completeness, or release signals. Evidence classes are accounting for what each organ proves, not scores. Nothing here authorizes release, hosted deployment, provider calls, source mutation, private-root equivalence, or proof authority above each organ's own claim ceiling.

## How to read a card

Each card carries: **What it makes visible** (plain language) · **For agents** (what to run it for) · **First command** (copyable) · **Does not authorize** (the claim ceiling) · **Wiring** (standalone or wired) · **Source relations** (body-free file/shard edge handle) · **Drill down** (paper module, standard, concept/mechanism row, validator command, acceptance receipt).

## Evidence classes

The evidence class tells you how strong a card's backing is. It is a claim-boundary legend, not a score.

| Evidence class | Strength | Claim ceiling | Counts as substrate progress |
|---|---|---|---|
| `algorithmic_projection` | 3/5 | projection mechanics only, not domain correctness | yes |
| `bounded_runtime_computation` | 4/5 | bounded in-process runtime computation only, not domain correctness, release, benchmark, or product authority | yes |
| `external_subprocess_witness` | 4/5 | tool witness only, not general proof authority | yes |
| `fixture_echo_smoke` | 1/5 | smoke/projection demo only, not behavioral validation, not safety validation, not benchmark scores, and not product progress evidence | no (regression/smoke) |
| `fixture_schema_replay` | 2/5 | schema/regression fixture only | no (regression/smoke) |
| `semantic_validator` | 5/5 | validates declared public contract only | yes |
| `verified_macro_body_import` | 5/5 | verified non-secret macro body import only, not source authority, private-root equivalence, release, or publication | yes |

## Families

Jump to a cluster, then open the cards inside it:

- [Entry & Reveal](#entry--reveal) (2) — Microcosm's entry point and what its short guided path actually proves.
- [Architecture & Navigation](#architecture--navigation) (10) — The kernel primitives, pattern binding, doctrine grammar, route plane, and standards that give the system its shape and make it navigable.
- [Formal Math & Proof](#formal-math--proof) (18) — The Lean/proof evidence pipeline: corpus readiness, premise retrieval, tactic routing, verifier trace repair, bounded witnesses, and certificates.
- [Agent Reliability & Safety Replays](#agent-reliability--safety-replays) (18) — Source-open replay specimens for agent failure modes: red-team monitors, sabotage, sandbox escape, prompt injection, tool authority, memory poisoning, benchmark gaming, route observability, and provider budgets.
- [Research & Science Replays](#research--science-replays) (8) — Replay specimens that project scientific and forecasting workflows: replication rubrics, spatial world models, materials-lab safety, mechanistic interpretability, and prediction reconciliation.
- [Import, Projection & Drift](#import-projection--drift) (21) — The membrane that brings non-secret macro substrate into the public tree and keeps projections honest instead of letting them drift from source.
- [Work, Landing & Continuity](#work-landing--continuity) (5) — How reversible work transactions are recorded, how dirty-tree landing decisions are made, and how detached runs resume.

## Entry & Reveal

*Microcosm's entry point and what its short guided path actually proves.* (2 organs)

### Cold Reader Route Map

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Entry & Reveal

- **Organ id:** `cold_reader_route_map`
- **Specialty:** `agent-entry` · `getting-started` · `interesting-parts` · `evaluation` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `3` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It checks Microcosm's "what do I run first" guided path so that every step on the cold-reader route map is backed by a real command, a public doc reference, and an evidence receipt instead of just prose promises. A newcomer can therefore trust that the suggested ten-minute first-run tour is actually wired and honestly labeled, with small verified counts shown as plain accounting rather than success badges.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to confirm the declared first-run route sequence starts with the front-door commands (tour, status card, proof lab) and is ordinal-sorted, that each route names a runnable command plus a public docs reference and resolves by route_id to at least one receipt ref in the route-receipts file, and that nothing carries release/provider overclaims or private source bodies.
- **First command:** `microcosm cold-reader-route-map run-route-map-bundle --input examples/cold_reader_route_map/exported_cold_reader_route_map_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/cold_reader_route_map --card`
- **Does not authorize:** It is projection-only metadata that validates the declared public route contract; it is not route-registry authority and authorizes no source mutation, provider calls, release/publication, financial advice, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness.
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes the first-screen card as a handoff and sequences/binds named sibling organs (tour, status, authority, workingness, explain, observe, compile) by requiring each route row to point at their commands and receipts. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

## Architecture & Navigation

*The kernel primitives, pattern binding, doctrine grammar, route plane, and standards that give the system its shape and make it navigable.* (10 organs)

### Pattern Binding Contract

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Architecture & Navigation

- **Organ id:** `pattern_binding_contract`
- **Specialty:** `architecture` · `navigation` · `doctrine`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `2` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It checks that each declared "pattern" (a reusable bit of system structure) is properly hooked up: it has the required fields, points at real source material by reference, names the rule it answers to and what it explicitly does NOT claim, has no duplicate pattern IDs, and leaks no secrets or private bodies into the public record. It produces a written receipt showing the overall pass/fail, which pattern rows are accepted versus rejected and why, and what each row is forbidden from claiming.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to validate that public pattern rows meet the declared binding contract (required fields, no duplicate pattern IDs, resolved reference capsules, no private-body or secret leakage, no public-leaf overclaim) and to read the pass/reject findings and per-row anti-claims from the emitted JSON receipt.
- **First command:** `microcosm pattern-route-readiness validate-bundle --input examples/pattern_binding_contract/exported_route_readiness_bundle --out /tmp/microcosm-pattern-route-readiness`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public pattern-binding/route-readiness contract; it does not certify the private pattern ledger, public release or hosted-public readiness, publication, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness, and it does not turn any mined pattern row into a standalone public leaf (selection stays organ-first and fixture-bound).
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes the route-readiness selector contract via pattern_route_readiness (row-to-organ router, route cards, decision matrix, dependency DAG, macro validation report) and imports the real pattern ledger + substrate bindings, atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
- **Source relations:** `38` source-module edges · `5` source files · `0` source shards · `5` target files · `0` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=pattern_binding_contract]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ pattern_binding_contract`
- **Wires to:** `navigation_hologram_route_plane`, `agent_route_observability_runtime`, `cold_reader_route_map`, `macro_projection_import_protocol`, `pattern_assimilation_step`, `voice_to_doctrine_self_improvement_loop`
- **Drill down:** paper module [`paper_modules/pattern_binding_contract.md`](paper_modules/pattern_binding_contract.md) · standard [`standards/std_microcosm_pattern_binding_contract.json`](standards/std_microcosm_pattern_binding_contract.json) · concept `organ_doctrine_row:pattern_binding_contract.concept_binding` · mechanism `organ_doctrine_row:pattern_binding_contract.mechanism_binding` · validator `python -m microcosm_core.organs.pattern_binding_contract validate --input fixtures/first_wave/pattern_binding_contract/input --out receipts/first_wave/pattern_binding_contract` · acceptance `core/acceptance/first_wave_acceptance.json::accepted_current_authority_organs[organ_id=pattern_binding_contract]`

### Pattern Assimilation Step

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Architecture & Navigation

- **Organ id:** `pattern_assimilation_step`
- **Specialty:** `architecture` · `navigation` · `doctrine`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** When a piece of work lands in the local substrate, this organ checks the closeout records for it — confirming that exactly one same-lane "what did we learn from this" decision was filed per landed item, that any claimed refinement names an owner-visible surface and the artifact it changed, that a "nothing to refine" entry carries its required typed fields, and that there are no duplicate or off-lane entries. It runs over fixture data and makes the closeout bookkeeping inspectable, showing whether the recorded closeouts conform to the system's stated learning-from-landed-work rules, rather than leaving that on faith.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to validate that each landed organ in the fixture set has exactly one same-lane closeout decision: either a concrete refinement receipt naming the owner surface and changed artifact, or a typed nothing_to_refine receipt with stewardship checked, next-best-lane checked, and a re-entry condition. It also checks residual lifecycle posture, rejects duplicate refinement receipt ids, rejects local-lesson authority upgrades to global doctrine, and rejects raw-seed bodies in fixtures. It asserts only that the declared closeout contract holds over fixtures, not that any live learning actually occurred.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.validators.acceptance --only pattern_assimilation_step --input fixtures/first_wave/pattern_assimilation_step/input --out receipts/first_wave/pattern_assimilation_acceptance.json`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public closeout contract over synthetic fixture data; it does not ingest private lessons, mutate live ledgers, promote global doctrine, authorize release or publication, make provider calls, claim private-data equivalence, or certify public runtime behavior.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Reads landed-organ closeout summaries and binds only to the shared acceptance-validator + evidence/authority spine; the paper module names no organ it feeds or consumes.
- **Source relations:** `74` source-module edges · `4` source files · `18` source shards · `4` target files · `18` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=pattern_assimilation_step]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ pattern_assimilation_step`

### Navigation Hologram Route Plane

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Architecture & Navigation

- **Organ id:** `navigation_hologram_route_plane`
- **Specialty:** `agent-entry` · `architecture` · `navigation` · `doctrine`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `5` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This checks that a folder's local "how to get around" surface behaves: the path starts from one control entry, then drills into browsable lists of routes and cards, and those browse rows are never allowed to pose as the source of truth. It makes visible, in plain receipt files, that stale or mislabeled navigation is caught, that compressed cards keep a note of what they left out, and that nothing private or secret leaks into the navigation material while moving around. It only inspects toy fixture files and exact copied-but-non-secret navigation source modules; it does not touch any live system.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to confirm a fixture's route-plane contract holds and that browse rows stay projections, not authority. The fixture run exercises eight expected negative cases and confirms each is detected: (1) stale source coupling bundled with a banned first-contact drilldown route, (2) a route card missing its omission receipt, (3) an atlas projection claiming control-entry authority, (4) a route card leaking private body content, (5) a route summary overclaiming freshness while coupling is stale, (6) a duplicate route id, (7) entry-payload compaction dropping a required control-floor field, and (8) an affordance-passport anti-trigger row that must be demoted before similarity can select it. A separate validate-route-plane-bundle subcommand additionally checks copied source-module digests and required anchors plus a secret-exclusion scan, with all body text kept out of the receipts.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.navigation_hologram_route_plane run --input fixtures/first_wave/navigation_hologram_route_plane/input --out receipts/first_wave/navigation_hologram_route_plane`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public toy route-plane contract and its regression fixtures (plus exact copied non-secret navigation source modules in the bundle path); it does not prove live route freshness, grant source authority, authorize any later organ, run any provider/live-kernel call, or certify the whole wave.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private/secret scans, receipts, schemas); its "feeds" entries are self-contained pattern assertions, not live consumption of other named organs. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

### Proof Diagnostic Evidence Spine

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** An evidence checkpoint that sits in front of formal-proof work. It reads the diagnostic records left by earlier proof-pipeline steps and writes a "diagnostic board" listing which checks were accepted, which were rejected, and why. The board shows exactly what evidence was kept, and refuses to let raw model output, a stale record, or a merely-passing check get inflated into a claim that the math is actually correct. It only organizes and judges existing records; it never runs a proof checker itself.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to turn upstream Ring2 proof-pipeline receipt references (failure-taxonomy, graph-update, verifier-trace repair, evidence-cell anchor) into accepted/rejected diagnostic receipts, and to confirm forbidden cases (provider proof bodies, source-authority upgrades, stale receipt coupling, runtime-correctness overclaims) are rejected as regression guards before any downstream proof-witness step. It emits a diagnostic board, proof receipts, a provider-payload policy result, and a proof-evidence validation receipt over receipt refs only.
- **First command:** `microcosm proof-diagnostic-evidence-spine run --input fixtures/first_wave/proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine/input --out receipts/first_wave/proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine`
- **Does not authorize:** It records proof/evidence diagnostics over existing receipt references only. It does not run Lean, call providers, expose proof bodies, turn a passing check into formal-proof or theorem authority, prove runtime or whole-system correctness, authorize later organs, certify public release, authorize publication or recipient work, or establish secret export.
- **Wiring:** wired — Consumes upstream receipts from formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver, and Ring2 failure-taxonomy/graph-update runs, and feeds the diagnostic board downstream toward formal_math_lean_proof_witness and the pattern-binding/doctrine-grammar boundary. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
- **Source relations:** `10` source-module edges · `1` source files · `2` source shards · `1` target files · `2` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine`
- **Wires to:** `formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop`, `formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver`, `formal_math_lean_proof_witness`, `verifier_lab_kernel`, `ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness`, `target_shape_tactic_routing_gate`

### Formal Math Readiness Gate

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `formal_math_readiness_gate`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Before anyone tries to prove a math theorem with the Lean prover, this gate reads simple description files that declare what a math setup is supposed to have — which math library is claimed to be present, which proof tactics are reported as already probed, which lemmas may be looked up, and which limits apply to the text budgets handed to AI providers — and writes a plain checklist of what is allowed to be attempted versus blocked. It works only from those declared description files; it does not inspect the real toolchain or run anything. Its guards keep the claims honest and checkable: it refuses to let a library be marked available unless a probe result backs that up, blocks routing a proof tactic that was not probed, and refuses to let any real proof text sneak into the lemma-lookup tables or provider budgets.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to get a machine-readable readiness board listing available vs. blocked tactics, allowed lemma-lookup rows, and admissible target-shape routes before deciding what a downstream prover step may safely attempt. The board is derived from declared metadata only, so treat it as a permission/boundary surface, not as evidence the environment actually works.
- **First command:** `microcosm formal-math-readiness-gate run --input fixtures/first_wave/formal_math_readiness_gate/input --out receipts/first_wave/formal_math_readiness_gate`
- **Does not authorize:** It only validates and projects declared readiness metadata; it does not run Lean/Lake, inspect the real toolchain, call providers, prove any theorem correct, produce benchmark scores, or authorize Mathlib-dependent proof attempts.
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes the macro intake cell formal_math_readiness_extensions and gates the still-bounded formal_math_lean_proof_witness (its next_boundary), atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
- **Source relations:** `91` source-module edges · `14` source files · `2` source shards · `14` target files · `2` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=formal_math_readiness_gate]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ formal_math_readiness_gate`

### Corpus Readiness Mathlib Absence Gate

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `corpus_readiness_mathlib_absence_gate`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`

- **What it makes visible:** It reads a recorded readiness report from a Lean math toolchain run and makes one fact inspectable: when the report was captured, the Mathlib library was not importable (its import probe failed). From that, it lists which math corpora are absent or usable only for translation smoke tests, and which downstream tasks must be blocked before any Mathlib-dependent proof work is attempted. It also re-checks that the recorded source files match their recorded SHA-256 digests and that no proof bodies, provider outputs, or private paths leaked into the public output. The result shows exactly where the proof pipeline draws a hard "not ready, do not proceed" line, with provenance, instead of quietly assuming the environment is fine.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to check, against copied non-secret readiness rows, whether the Mathlib import probe passed and therefore which consumer cases are allowed versus blocked before routing proof or premise work. It validates and projects the recorded accounting and, when a Lean toolchain is present, runs a bounded Std/Mathlib import probe; it does not run a full Lake build or prove anything.

### Mathematical Strategy Atlas Hypothesis Scorer

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `mathematical_strategy_atlas_hypothesis_scorer`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `10` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Before any proof is attempted, this organ looks at a math problem's feature tags and writes down its first-guess strategy (for example, "this looks like an if-and-only-if, so split it both ways"), and flags anything it cannot map as an explicit "no strategy matched" instead of a silent failure. The chosen opening move, why it was chosen, and the cases it could not map are all recorded in machine-readable receipts.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to deterministically pick a pre-proof strategy hypothesis and retrieval terms from problem feature tags (highest feature overlap wins; zero overlap becomes a typed strategy-selection miss), and to check that unknown strategy ids, proof bodies, oracle labels, post-oracle selection, and release/proof/provider overclaims are all rejected.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.mathematical_strategy_atlas_hypothesis_scorer run --input fixtures/first_wave/mathematical_strategy_atlas_hypothesis_scorer/input --out receipts/first_wave/mathematical_strategy_atlas_hypothesis_scorer`
- **Does not authorize:** It only projects pre-oracle strategy-hypothesis and retrieval mechanics; it does not run Lean/Lake, prove theorems, establish domain or theorem correctness, reveal oracle labels, expose proof bodies, call providers, tune on test answers, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus its own copied-body source manifest; no other named organ feeds or consumes it.

### Tactic Portfolio Availability Probe

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `tactic_portfolio_availability_probe`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It turns one captured Lean run's results into an inspectable list of which proof shortcuts ("tactics" like `rfl`, `simp`, `omega`, `aesop`) were recorded as compiling, showing at a glance which are usable and which are off before anything treats a tactic as available. In this fixture seven tactics are marked usable and `aesop` is marked failed (its recorded run hit a missing-Mathlib error). The tool reads pre-recorded status rows and checks them for honesty; it does not run Lean itself.
- **For agents:** An agent reads it to get an environment-scoped map of which tactics were recorded as compiling, and relies on its checks: every tactic must carry a compile status, no Mathlib-dependent tactic may be marked available unless the Mathlib import probe passed, and any tactic a consumer references must exist in the probed portfolio.

- **Does not authorize:** It only projects and validates which tactics were recorded as compiling in one captured environment; it does not run Lean/Lake at all, prove any goal, certify domain correctness, call providers, claim benchmark performance, or authorize release.

### Target Shape Tactic Routing Gate

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `target_shape_tactic_routing_gate`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Before a proof is attempted, this organ checks a list of candidate proof tactics for a given goal and writes down an allow-or-reject decision, with a plain reason, for each one. Rejections fall into three kinds: the tactic isn't actually available in the declared environment, it was never listed in the environment's tested set of tactics, or it simply doesn't fit the kind of goal being proved. The resulting record shows, tactic by tactic, exactly what was admitted or blocked and why, instead of an opaque "we tried these" claim. It only inspects and records the routing decision over references that already exist; it never runs a prover or proves anything itself.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to produce an auditable per-tactic decision record (admissible / unavailable / unprobed / shape-inadmissible, each with a reason) over real Ring2 route references, asserting the decision is made before any proof execution. It is a checker/projector of the pre-execution routing decision, not a live prover gate: it reads supplied route refs and emits allow/reject decisions plus authority-ceiling and secret-exclusion receipts; it does not run Lean/Lake or attempt a proof.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.cli target-shape-tactic-routing-gate run-routing-bundle --input examples/target_shape_tactic_routing_gate/exported_target_shape_tactic_routing_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/target_shape_tactic_routing_gate`
- **Does not authorize:** It only inspects and records the projection mechanics of pre-execution tactic-routing references — emitting per-tactic allow/reject decisions with reasons. It does not run Lean/Lake, does not prove or judge the correctness of any goal, emits no proof bodies, makes no provider calls, performs no post-execution route selection, reports no benchmark scores or maturity, and authorizes no release.
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes/anchors against receipts of formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver, and proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine over the same Ring2 run
- **Source relations:** `45` source-module edges · `5` source files · `2` source shards · `5` target files · `2` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=target_shape_tactic_routing_gate]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ target_shape_tactic_routing_gate`
- **Wires to:** `formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop`, `formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver`, `proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine`, `tactic_portfolio_availability_probe`, `verifier_lab_kernel`

### Undeclared Library Prior Symbol Classifier

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `2` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It checks whether a Lean proof cites a library result (a lemma or definition) that was never on its approved list. Even after a prover accepts a proof, that proof can still quietly use a library symbol it wasn't allowed to, and this organ surfaces those out-of-bounds uses as an inspectable record that names each symbol and where the rule came from. It matters because "the proof checked" does not mean "the proof stayed within the allowed set of building blocks," and this makes that gap visible without ever reading the proof's own steps.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it over symbol-observation rows whose qualified references are already extracted, scoring each known symbol against the allowed premise set and getting a classification: UNDECLARED_LIBRARY_PRIOR (route bridge_escalate) when a known library symbol is outside the allowed ids, or PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION (route retry) which takes precedence when cited-unallowed ids are present. It also emits a secret-exclusion scan confirming no proof bodies, private source refs, oracle ids, or provider payloads entered the receipts.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier run --input fixtures/first_wave/undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier/input --out receipts/first_wave/undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier`
- **Does not authorize:** It only projects the symbol-boundary classification mechanic over copied non-secret Lean/Std premise rows and pre-extracted symbol observations; it does not read proof source, run Lean or Lake, prove theorem correctness, treat the whole standard library as an implicit allowlist, claim Mathlib availability, call providers, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes the real Ring2 premise-index substrate and anchors the Mathlib-absent boundary on the corpus_readiness_mathlib_absence_gate and tactic_portfolio_availability_probe receipts
- **Source relations:** `39` source-module edges · `6` source files · `28` source shards · `6` target files · `28` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ undeclared_library_prior_symbol_classifier`

### Ring2 Premise Retrieval Precision Recall Harness

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** When a math-proving system searches for the supporting facts ("premises") a proof will need, this organ replays saved records of that search and reports, problem by problem, how much of the needed support the search actually turned up. Per problem it labels one of four outcomes: the search found everything needed and the proof went through; it found everything needed but the proof still failed; it found only some of the needed support; or it found none of it. Separating "the proof failed even though every needed premise was found" from "the proof failed because the search missed a needed premise" shows which part to fix. It also runs as a regression guard that refuses inputs which try to slip the answer into the search itself (the known-correct premises planted in the ranked results), leak proof text, tune on the test answers, or claim more than retrieval-quality numbers.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to compute per-problem precision@k / recall@k over copied Ring-2 retrieval rankings and bucket each problem into retrieval_hit / partial_retrieval_miss / retrieval_miss / proof_failure_despite_hit, plus aggregate hit-over-candidate precision and hit-over-needed recall. The validator emits blocking findings (status blocked, non-zero exit) for oracle/needed premise ids planted in rankings, leaked proof/provider/private body fields, test-split tuning flags, metric overclaims (proof/benchmark/provider/release authority), a missing adversarial-decoy case, incomplete copied-material provenance, and source-artifact digest mismatches; receipts carry import ids/digests only, never proof bodies.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness run --input fixtures/first_wave/ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness/input --out receipts/first_wave/ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness`
- **Does not authorize:** These are after-the-fact retrieval-attribution labels and precision/recall counts over copied run records only. The organ does not run Lean or Lake, call any provider, expose proof bodies, tune on test answers, claim benchmark performance, prove theorem correctness, or authorize release, and its labels are explicitly forbidden from flowing into provider context. The aggregate numbers describe only the copied fixture/bundle replayed, not any benchmark standing.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus digest-verified copied source artifacts; the paper module names no consuming or feeding organ.
- **Source relations:** `29` source-module edges · `1` source files · `4` source shards · `1` target files · `4` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness`
- **Wires to:** `mathematical_strategy_atlas_hypothesis_scorer`, `verifier_lab_kernel`
- **Drill down:** paper module [`paper_modules/ring2_premise_precision_recall.md`](paper_modules/ring2_premise_precision_recall.md) · standard [`standards/std_microcosm_ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness.json`](standards/std_microcosm_ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness.json) · concept `organ_doctrine_row:ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness.concept_binding` · mechanism `organ_doctrine_row:ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness.mechanism_binding` · validator `python -m microcosm_core.organs.ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness run --input fixtures/first_wave/ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness/input --out receipts/first_wave/ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness` · acceptance `core/acceptance/first_wave_acceptance.json::accepted_current_authority_organs[organ_id=ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness]`

### Formal Math Lean Proof Witness

`external_subprocess_witness` · strength 4/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `formal_math_lean_proof_witness`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This takes a small, purpose-built Lean math file (a handful of toy theorems written just for this demo) and a tiny project setup, copies them into a throwaway scratch folder, and actually tries to compile them with the installed Lean theorem-prover and its Lake build tool. It then writes down exactly what happened: whether the Lean and Lake tools were found, whether the build passed, fingerprints (hashes) of the source files, the names of the theorems it defined, and how many lines each file had. It also deliberately feeds in a broken proof and a couple of off-limits files to confirm they get rejected. The point is to show real proof-checking machinery run on a small example, while keeping the written records honest and redacted: no proof text or internal logs leak out, and it states plainly that this is a narrow toy check on one fixture, not a general-purpose proof system.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to get a redacted receipt confirming that a small public Lean fixture compiles under the locally installed Lean/Lake toolchain, and that the guardrails fire on the negative cases: Mathlib/Aesop/Batteries imports are rejected, private source refs and embedded proof bodies in manifests are refused, and an intentionally invalid Lean proof is rejected. The receipt carries tool-availability status, Lake build status, source hashes, declaration names, line counts, and negative-case coverage only; it never exports proof bodies, stdout/stderr text, or provider payloads.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.formal_math_lean_proof_witness run --input fixtures/first_wave/formal_math_lean_proof_witness/input --out receipts/first_wave/formal_math_lean_proof_witness`
- **Does not authorize:** It authorizes only a witness that a tiny declared public toy proof compiled under the locally installed Lean/Lake toolchain in a temporary workspace, plus confirmation that its leakage guardrails fired. It does not authorize Mathlib/Aesop/Batteries-dependent or general proof or theorem-program authority, provider calls, private proof import, benchmark or performance claims, whole-system correctness, or any release, hosted deployment, or publication.

### Verifier Lab Kernel

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `verifier_lab_kernel`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`

- **What it makes visible:** This assembly point for the Lean/proof toolkit runs nine smaller formal-math pipeline checkers together and folds their results into one leak-proof report that labels every line by where it came from: a Lean verifier, an answer-key (oracle) comparator, an AI suggestion, a retrieval miss, or a row thrown out for breaking the rules. The report carries only references, hashes, counts, and verdicts, never the actual proof text, AI output, or answer-key bodies. One receipt shows which claims a Lean verifier actually backed versus which are just hints or were rejected, instead of leaving a pile of separate outputs to be taken on faith.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to compose the nine public formal-math organs into one receipt and confirm the proof packet obeys its declared contract, keeping verifier-backed, oracle-compared, provider-suggested, and contract-rejected rows in separate buckets, with proof/provider/oracle bodies excluded from the receipt.

- **Does not authorize:** It validates the declared public contract shape of the proof packet and component receipts only; it does not prove anything correct, count oracle/provider output as forward proof success, import private or Mathlib-dependent proof bodies, call providers, mutate source, or claim benchmark solve rates, release, or maturity.
- **Wiring:** wired — composition root that runs and folds receipts from nine named formal-math organs (corpus_readiness_mathlib_absence_gate, lean_std_premise_index, formal_math_premise_retrieval, tactic_portfolio_availability_probe, target_shape_tactic_routing_gate, ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness, formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine, formal_math_lean_proof_witness)

### Verifier Lab Execution Spine

`external_subprocess_witness` · strength 4/5 · Formal Math & Proof

- **Organ id:** `verifier_lab_execution_spine`
- **Specialty:** `formal-methods` · `theorem-proving` · `lean` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `2` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It copies a small public Lean math project into a throwaway temporary workspace and actually runs the Lean/Lake checker on a handful of small, bounded proof-step attempts the tool builds itself. It then writes down what the checker said: which attempts were accepted, which failed, and the failure category for each, plus safety counts (for example, how many attempts tried to sneak in forbidden content and were rejected). The pass/fail facts and the safety counts are readable directly, while the tool never shows the underlying proof text, never calls any outside service, and never modifies the original project or any existing source files.
- **For agents:** Run it for a structured, leak-proof receipt of which bounded Lean transition candidates the local Lean/Lake checker (run on a temp copy of a public fixture) accepted or rejected, with residual-retry and bounded policy reruns re-checked the same way. Proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, and provider/oracle text are excluded (proof_body_exported=False, body_in_receipt=False); the receipt carries only ids, action classes, Lean return codes, accept/residual verdicts, safety counters, authority ceiling, and anti-claim.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.verifier_lab_execution_spine run --input fixtures/first_wave/verifier_lab_execution_spine/input --out .microcosm/verifier_lab_execution_spine`
- **Does not authorize:** It is a tool-witness receipt for bounded public Lean transition rows only: it does not establish general proof authority, count oracle/provider output as proof, export proof bodies or tactic scripts, call providers, mutate source, claim benchmark solve-rates, or authorize release/publication.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Self-contained Lean-execution replay specimen over its own fixture; binds only to the shared kernel + secret-exclusion/receipts/authority spine.
- **Source relations:** `100` source-module edges · `5` source files · `15` source shards · `5` target files · `15` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=verifier_lab_execution_spine]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ verifier_lab_execution_spine`

### Agent Monitor Redteam Falsification Replay

`bounded_runtime_computation` · strength 4/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `agent_monitor_redteam_falsification_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming`
- **Substrate disposition:** `retained_regression_validator` · real bodies `0` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `retained_regression_negative_wrapper_only`
- **What it makes visible:** It replays three made-up agent trajectories (a normal one, one trying to hide a failure, and one overstepping a tool's permissions) and checks that every monitor verdict — including a clean "pass" — comes with the receipts needed to re-run and verify it. The receipts show exactly which checks each verdict depends on, and any verdict missing its backing evidence gets quarantined instead of trusted. It is a demo over fictional data, not a measurement of how well a real monitor catches anything.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to validate a public monitor-redteam bundle: the runner verifies copied macro-pattern bodies and a sanitized dogfood trace slice by digest, recomputes monitor verdict spans from public evidence refs, rejects verdict/source-evidence/body-in-receipt/digest tampering, and keeps all receipts body-free while refusing live-agent, credential, exploit, private-reasoning, traffic, and performance-claim inputs.
- **First command:** `microcosm agent-monitor-redteam-falsification-replay run-monitor-bundle --input examples/agent_monitor_redteam_falsification_replay/exported_monitor_redteam_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/agent_monitor_redteam_falsification_replay`
- **Does not authorize:** Bounded public runtime validation over copied non-secret macro pattern bodies, sanitized dogfood trace slices, recomputed monitor-verdict spans, source-artifact evidence refs, digest/body-free/private-state gates, and negative cases only; no live agent execution, monitor product performance, control-eval score, safety-validation, benchmark, provider-call, source-mutation, release, publication, or product authority.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel plus evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, source-module digest checks, public trace recomputation, receipts, authority_ceiling); it remains a drilldown, not product-path live monitor authority. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

### Agent Memory Temporal Conflict Replay

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming` · `security`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Replays a canned three-step story: an agent's memory first records two facts (a preference and a tool result), then learns one is now out of date and edits the preference and deletes the stale fact, then re-runs the same task once with memory on and once with memory off. From plain receipts, it shows that the update and deletion each carry a "conflict" and "downgrade" receipt before the memory is allowed to affect the answer, that the memory-on vs memory-off runs are compared through logged evidence rather than just the final wording, and that private-thread content stays in the record only as metadata pointers, never as copied text. It checks the bookkeeping of this synthetic example; it does not judge whether the agent's memory decisions were the right ones.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this against a synthetic fixture to see the shape of the memory-update bookkeeping it should produce: updates and deletes of older memory must carry temporal conflict-edge and stale-downgrade refs before they may affect a replay; private threads appear only as metadata-only refs with no exported body; and a memory-on replay must cite evidence handles while the memory-on/off pair is reconciled by an answer-delta receipt, not by final-answer comparison alone. It also confirms seven failure fixtures (raw transcript export, private-candidate auto-promotion, stale-preference override, memory-as-source-authority, vector recall without an evidence handle, final-answer-only credit, active-injection authority) are all rejected. It validates structure and receipt presence on fixture rows, not the correctness of any real memory system.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay/input --out /tmp/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay_out`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates the projection mechanics of a synthetic memory fixture only — that the required refs, decisions, paired replays, negative cases, and secret-exclusion scan line up and that receipts are body-free. It does not claim live-memory product quality, judge whether memory decisions were domain-correct, treat memory recall as source authority, adopt active injection, export private transcripts, call providers, mutate source, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, schemas, and a public execution-trace builder), not to runtime invocation of other named organs
- **Source relations:** `40` source-module edges · `5` source files · `0` source shards · `5` target files · `0` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay`
- **Drill down:** paper module [`paper_modules/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.md`](paper_modules/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.md) · standard [`standards/std_microcosm_agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.json`](standards/std_microcosm_agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.json) · concept `organ_doctrine_row:agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.concept_binding` · mechanism `organ_doctrine_row:agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay.mechanism_binding` · validator `python -m microcosm_core.organs.agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay` · acceptance `core/acceptance/first_wave_acceptance.json::accepted_current_authority_organs[organ_id=agent_memory_temporal_conflict_replay]`

### Sleeper Memory Poisoning Quarantine Replay

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming` · `security`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `6` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This is a worked, made-up example of how an agent should handle a tampered "memory": a poisoned note gets spotted, held in quarantine, blocked from being used in any later action, and finally deleted-with-an-audit, with a clean re-run recorded showing the bad memory is no longer there. The organ does not actually delete anything or perform the re-run; it reads the on-disk record of that whole guard-and-cleanup sequence and checks that every required step and receipt lines up, so exactly which checks must hold is visible. It works entirely from synthetic refs and metadata, with no private memory or transcripts exposed.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to check that a synthetic memory-poisoning handling record has all the required pieces (provenance-bound write proposals, a quarantine verdict on the untrusted poisoned source, a retrieval-blocked-before-action gate, and a rollback with a deletion-audit ref plus a cold-rerun receipt marking the memory absent), that a body-free private-state scan finds no leaks, and that all eight known bad patterns (e.g. private-body export, live-user-memory claim, trusted promotion from untrusted context, deletion without audit) are each rejected with the expected error codes.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay --acceptance-out receipts/acceptance/first_wave/sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay_fixture_acceptance.json`
- **Does not authorize:** It only checks the structural shape and internal consistency of a synthetic memory-security policy projection recorded as JSON. It does not run or validate any real memory store, does not itself quarantine, delete, or re-run anything, and does not prove that any system actually resists poisoning. It exports no private memory bodies or transcripts, calls no providers, mutates no source, produces no benchmark scores, and authorizes no release (all authority-ceiling flags are hardcoded false).
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas, and the shared forbidden-classes policy)
- **Source relations:** `229` source-module edges · `7` source files · `30` source shards · `7` target files · `30` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ sleeper_memory_poisoning_quarantine_replay`

### MCP Tool Authority Replay

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `mcp_tool_authority_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming` · `provider-ops`

- **What it makes visible:** When an AI agent uses outside tools (look something up, change a ticket, read a result from an untrusted source), this checks a recorded log of that tool use to confirm each action was properly fenced: bound to a narrow permission, approved before it changed anything, given a way to undo it, and kept from letting untrusted tool output boss the agent around. It runs on a small built-in make-believe example that carries only labels and references (no real accounts, secrets, or tool contents), so the safety checks an agent's tool use is supposed to pass are inspectable without anything touching a live account.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to check that a recorded tool-use bundle is admissible: every call has a narrow scope ref, writes carry approval plus ledger plus rollback refs, untrusted output is treated as data not instruction, cold-replay receipts exist, payloads/account refs stay metadata-only, and all eight expected negative-abuse cases fire. It validates presence and consistency of this evidence only; it does not run tools, hit providers, or authorize anything.

- **Does not authorize:** It only checks that the tool-authority evidence in a recorded bundle (scopes, approvals, rollbacks, instruction/data splits, cold replays, redaction, and the expected abuse-case failures) is present and internally consistent. It does not run tools or authorize live MCP/account access, credential or payload export, treating tool output as instruction, source mutation, benchmark safety scores, or release, and it makes no claim that the underlying tool-use policy is domain-correct.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, and the shared public agent-execution-trace macro tool); no other named organ is fed or consumed

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `3` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Replays a recorded sample agent episode (built from synthetic, body-free rows) and makes visible whether the instructions the agent treated as trusted were kept separate from untrusted web, tool, or browser text before any sensitive action was taken. Row by row, the record shows where untrusted text flowed, what the policy decided for each flow (allow / warn / block / review) before the action, and that the recorded outcome leaked no secret and disclosed no trusted context. It also bundles deliberately-bad cases it must reject (e.g. untrusted text reaching a sensitive action ungated, or a credential being exfiltrated).
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to confirm that, in this replayed synthetic episode, every recorded flow from untrusted source text to a privileged action was gated (blocked, sent to review, or sanitized) before the action, and that the recorded outputs disclosed no trusted context or credential. Evidence is the validated fixture rows plus a body-free agent-execution trace, not a live agent run or a general robustness guarantee.
- **First command:** `microcosm indirect-prompt-injection-information-flow-policy-replay run-prompt-injection-bundle --input examples/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay/exported_prompt_injection_flow_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay`
- **Does not authorize:** Passing receipts only show this projection satisfies the named information-flow contract over synthetic, redacted, body-free rows; they do not prove general prompt-injection robustness, benchmark performance, live account/tool/provider safety, hidden-message handling in a real system, source-mutation authority, or release authority.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts) plus an internal agent_execution_trace macro refactor, not other organs
- **Source relations:** `185` source-module edges · `5` source files · `22` source shards · `5` target files · `22` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay`
- **Drill down:** paper module [`paper_modules/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.md`](paper_modules/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.md) · standard [`standards/std_microcosm_indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.json`](standards/std_microcosm_indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.json) · concept `organ_doctrine_row:indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.concept_binding` · mechanism `organ_doctrine_row:indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay.mechanism_binding` · validator `python -m microcosm_core.organs.indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay --acceptance-out receipts/acceptance/first_wave/indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay_fixture_acceptance.json` · acceptance `core/acceptance/first_wave_acceptance.json::accepted_current_authority_organs[organ_id=indirect_prompt_injection_information_flow_policy_replay]`

### Agentic Vulnerability Discovery Patch Proof Replay

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `6` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Takes a claim that an AI agent "found and fixed a security bug" and lays it out as a local, inspectable chain of made-up (synthetic) evidence: the imagined target, the suspected issue, the trace pointed to as backing, a reference to an abstract exploitability argument, the patch, and regression tests the fixture says fail before the fix and pass after it. The organ checks only that these pieces are all present, refer to each other consistently, and carry no real targets, exploits, payloads, credentials, or attack steps; it does not run the tests or judge whether the bug or fix is actually real. The receipt shows whether the declared chain holds together, with no real attack material ever present.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to check that a vulnerability-discovery-and-patch claim carries a structurally complete, body-free, secret-free evidence chain (target to hypothesis to trace to abstract-exploitability ref to patch to declared-passing regression tests to sandbox verdict to cold replay) with every stage present and cross-referenced, before treating the claim as inspectable. It validates declared structure and the secret/live-access exclusion only; it does not execute tests, exploit anything, or judge real-world correctness.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay --acceptance-out receipts/acceptance/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay_fixture_acceptance.json`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the projection/evidence-chain mechanics of a synthetic replay: structural presence, cross-reference consistency, declared boolean flags, and the secret/live-access exclusion scan. It does not execute the regression tests and makes no judgment of real-world security or fix correctness. It authorizes no live-target testing, real CVE exploitation, weaponized payloads, credential handling, network exfiltration, actionable exploit steps, provider calls, source mutation, benchmark security scores, release, or any whole-system security claim.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Self-contained replay specimen; consumes the shared public agent-execution-trace refactor (build_public_agentic_vulnerability_patch_proof_trace) and binds to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine, not to runtime invocation of other named organs.
- **Source relations:** `285` source-module edges · `9` source files · `36` source shards · `9` target files · `36` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay`
- **Drill down:** paper module [`paper_modules/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.md`](paper_modules/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.md) · standard [`standards/std_microcosm_agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.json`](standards/std_microcosm_agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.json) · concept `organ_doctrine_row:agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.concept_binding` · mechanism `organ_doctrine_row:agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay.mechanism_binding` · validator `python -m microcosm_core.organs.agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay --acceptance-out receipts/acceptance/first_wave/agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay_fixture_acceptance.json` · acceptance `core/acceptance/first_wave_acceptance.json::accepted_current_authority_organs[organ_id=agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay]`

### Agent Route Observability Runtime

### Provider Context Recipe Budget Policy

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Agent Reliability & Safety Replays

- **Organ id:** `provider_context_recipe_budget_policy`
- **Specialty:** `ai-safety` · `agent-evaluation` · `red-teaming` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `14` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This organ checks that the bundles of context an AI agent would assemble before calling an outside model provider stay inside fixed size limits (in bytes), fill their sections in the declared order until the budget runs out, list any section that was dropped for not fitting, and never carry answer keys, proof solutions, or other "correct answer" material. The record shows the exact size ceilings for each recipe, which sections fit versus got left out, and which output each recipe is allowed to produce, so the context boundary is inspectable as plain accounting before any provider call or answer authority is ever in play. It only validates this metadata; it does not itself call any provider.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to validate that its planned provider-context recipes respect their fixed byte budgets, fill sections in declared order under those budgets, emit an omitted-sections manifest whenever something is dropped, and route to the correct deliverable type, while rejecting any recipe that authorizes a provider call or carries truth-side, oracle, or proof-body material. It also digest-checks the copied standard/source-module bodies it ships. Output is context metadata and verdicts only.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.provider_context_recipe_budget_policy run --input fixtures/first_wave/provider_context_recipe_budget_policy/input --out receipts/first_wave/provider_context_recipe_budget_policy`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates context-budget projection mechanics (byte ceilings, ordered section fill, omitted-section manifests, deliverable routing, and digest-checked source-body imports) only. It does not authorize provider/API calls, run Lean/Lake, expose or carry proof or oracle truth-side material, assert theorem or domain correctness, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas, authority ceiling); it copies standard/source-module bodies by digest but does not feed or consume other named organs at runtime.
- **Source relations:** `64` source-module edges · `8` source files · `48` source shards · `8` target files · `48` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=provider_context_recipe_budget_policy]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ provider_context_recipe_budget_policy`
- **Wires to:** `bounded_autonomy_campaign_packet`, `tool_server_pressure_inventory`

## Research & Science Replays

*Replay specimens that project scientific and forecasting workflows: replication rubrics, spatial world models, materials-lab safety, mechanistic interpretability, and prediction reconciliation.* (8 organs)

### Research Replication Rubric Artifact Replay

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Research & Science Replays

- **Organ id:** `research_replication_rubric_artifact_replay`
- **Specialty:** `research-workflows` · `forecasting` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `1` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** It checks whether a claim that an AI agent "replicated a research paper" comes with the paper trail real replication would leave behind. It re-runs nothing; instead it confirms the bundle names every required piece of evidence: a breakdown of the paper's contributions, a grading rubric, the list of allowed public inputs, a from-scratch repo scaffold, an experiment plan, the metric scripts, a roster of declared file-hashes for the outputs plus hashes that all stay inside that roster, a grader report, a capped compute/runtime budget, an ablation diff, a failure list, and a cold-rerun receipt. It also catches eight ways a claim can cheat: reusing the original authors' code, leaking a hidden rubric, calling a run a "success" when only a write-up backs it, asserting a benchmark score, leaking a private paper or dataset body, using unbounded compute, grading only the final answer, or pointing at a file-hash that was never declared. The work runs on two made-up sample papers (one machine-learning method, one computational-science study), and the generated receipt shows which of the eight cheats each test case triggered, rather than taking "it was replicated" on trust.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to validate a replication-claim bundle: that the two synthetic paper capsules each carry all fourteen required evidence references (rubric tree, contribution decomposition, allowed-input refs, scaffold, experiment DAG, metric scripts, declared+actual artifact-hash refs, grader report, budget, ablation diff, failure taxonomy, cold-rerun receipt), that the policy forbids the prohibited shortcuts, and that all eight negative fixtures fire their expected error codes (author-code reuse, hidden-rubric leakage, report-only success, benchmark overclaim, private-body leak, unbounded compute, final-answer-only grading, undeclared artifact hash). It validates reference presence and receipt shape only; it does not execute the scripts, run a rerun, or call providers.
- **First command:** `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m microcosm_core.organs.research_replication_rubric_artifact_replay run --input fixtures/first_wave/research_replication_rubric_artifact_replay/input --out receipts/first_wave/research_replication_rubric_artifact_replay`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates the shape and presence of synthetic replay metadata and receipt references only - it does not run any experiment, metric script, or rerun, does not authorize any claim that a paper was actually replicated, that a benchmark score was achieved, or that the underlying science is correct, and it never calls providers, exposes private paper/data bodies, or authorizes publication or release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, schemas, and the shared public-agent-execution-trace macro tool); does not invoke another named organ at runtime. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

### Spatial World Model Counterfactual Simulation Replay

`bounded_runtime_computation` · strength 4/5 · Research & Science Replays

- **Organ id:** `spatial_world_model_counterfactual_simulation_replay`
- **Specialty:** `research-workflows` · `forecasting`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `3` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This replay takes six made-up "what if" spatial scenes from robotics and self-driving-style settings (a forklift appears from behind an occlusion, a small pedestrian steps into a crosswalk, a gust pushes a drone off course, a shiny floor fools a robot into seeing free space, a stacked load shifts into a lane, and an oncoming car turns late) and shows each one as inspectable rows: the starting scene, the action taken, the predicted next scene, what changed between them, a sanity check, and honest notes on its limits (it is synthetic, not real-world ground truth). The rows show exactly what a spatial "world model" claim is built from, plus a checklist of dangerous claims it deliberately refuses to make.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to validate that the synthetic counterfactual-replay rows are well-formed (each cites scene-state, action-trace, predicted-state, transition-diff, oracle-check, and public sensor-packet refs plus limitation labels) and that every forbidden claim (private video export, raw sensor export, live robot/AV operation, real-world geographic accuracy, simulator-product claim, generated-video-only authority, benchmark scores, release) is rejected by a negative-case receipt.
- **First command:** `microcosm spatial-world-model-counterfactual-simulation-replay run-simulation-bundle --input examples/spatial_world_model_counterfactual_simulation_replay/exported_spatial_world_model_simulation_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/spatial_world_model_counterfactual_simulation_replay`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public contract of synthetic spatial counterfactual-replay metadata rows; it does not operate robots or AVs, claim real-world geographic accuracy, sell or validate a simulator product, treat generated video as authority, report benchmark scores, call providers, publish, host, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, public payload boundary, receipts), pulling in a copied non-secret macro source body but feeding no other named organ
- **Source relations:** `19` source-module edges · `3` source files · `13` source shards · `3` target files · `13` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=spatial_world_model_counterfactual_simulation_replay]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ spatial_world_model_counterfactual_simulation_replay`
