## Import, Projection & Drift

*The membrane that brings non-secret macro substrate into the public tree and keeps projections honest instead of letting them drift from source.* (21 organs)

### Macro Projection Import Protocol

`verified_macro_body_import` · strength 5/5 · Import, Projection & Drift

- **Organ id:** `macro_projection_import_protocol`
- **Specialty:** `source-import` · `provenance` · `drift-control`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `168` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This is the checkpoint that handles bringing material from the larger private project into the public Microcosm folder. When someone proposes a batch of files to import, it verifies each one: it only accepts non-secret material, and only when the destination file, a content fingerprint (to confirm the copy matches), a record of where it came from, and the supporting checks all line up. Anything held back as private or secret has to come with a written note saying so, and attempts to claim more authority than allowed are rejected. The record shows exactly what was imported, what was deliberately left out, and what was refused, so the public copy stays honest about its limits instead of quietly leaking private source or pretending to be more than it is.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to preview or validate a projection bundle before import: confirm each proposed cell has source and public-target refs, a matching content digest, omission receipts for any withheld private material, a body-free receipt contract, and an authority ceiling that stays capped below release. Use `plan` for a no-write preview (emits the intake-preview schema and per-cell ready/blocked status); use `run-projection-bundle` to validate-and-record.
- **First command:** `microcosm macro-projection-import-protocol plan --input examples/macro_projection_import_protocol/exported_projection_import_bundle`
- **Does not authorize:** It authorizes only verified non-secret macro body import with provenance and content-digest checks; it does not grant source authority, private-root equivalence, release, hosted deployment, publication, recipient work, provider or Lean/Lake execution, secret or private-source-body export, or any whole-system correctness claim.
- **Wiring:** wired — feeds macro_body_import_floor into microcosm status/spine, bridges via microcosm intake to reveal/evidence, and coordinates per-cell import statuses across named organs (e.g. formal_math_readiness_gate, agent_route_observability_runtime, mission_transaction_work_spine). Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

### World Model Projection Drift Control Room

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Import, Projection & Drift

- **Organ id:** `world_model_projection_drift_control_room`
- **Specialty:** `source-import` · `provenance` · `drift-control` · `provider-ops`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `4` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This organ shows, in plain receipts, where a projected copy of a world model has drifted from its real source. For each drift it names the signal, points to where the real source lives, gives a suggested repair route, and cites the test that would confirm the fix. The receipts show the drift is being flagged honestly: the projection never claims to be the source of truth, and the receipts deliberately leave out any private or credentialed data.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this to validate that a set of body-free drift rows each cite a source ref, repair route, validation ref, and target ref while keeping the forbidden booleans (source-authority, live-repair, source-mutation, doctrine-promotion, provider-export, private-export, release) false, and that the secret-exclusion scan passes with no body text emitted. The organ's fixture mode additionally asserts that the eight expected negative cases (missing source ref, missing validation ref, source-authority claim, live-repair authorization, private-runtime export, provider-payload export, automatic doctrine promotion, release claim) are rejected; the validation receipt records 8/8.
- **First command:** `microcosm world-model-projection-drift-control-room run-drift-control-bundle --input examples/world_model_projection_drift_control_room/exported_projection_drift_control_bundle --out receipts/runtime_shell/demo_project/organs/world_model_projection_drift_control_room`
- **Does not authorize:** It only validates the declared public, body-free drift-receipt contract; it does not inspect private runtime bodies, mutate or claim authority over source, repair live routes, promote doctrine, export provider payloads, or authorize publication or release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

## Work, Landing & Continuity

*How reversible work transactions are recorded, how dirty-tree landing decisions are made, and how detached runs resume.* (5 organs)

### Mission Transaction Work Spine

`algorithmic_projection` · strength 3/5 · Work, Landing & Continuity

- **Organ id:** `mission_transaction_work_spine`
- **Specialty:** `agent-concurrency` · `workflow-engineering` · `continuity` · `work-ledger`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `9` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Replays a fixed set of pre-recorded work-landing situations against a toy repository and shows when a change would be allowed to "land" versus blocked: two claims competing on the same file, a claim built on a stale parent commit, a claim missing its owned path, a clean preflight check that wrongly says the work is already finished, and which commit lane (a narrow scoped commit vs a broad checkpoint) a dirty working tree is permitted to use. Its exported bundle also anchors the public Work Ledger seed-speed source imports for session heartbeat, mutation-check, active-claim snapshot, and path-collision handling. The resulting receipts show exactly why each situation was permitted or refused, instead of an opaque "it's done" message.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to confirm that the projected work-landing, claim, checkpoint-lane, and Work Ledger seed-speed source-import metadata obeys the expected rules and emits the expected blocking error codes (e.g. SAME_PATH_CLAIM_CONFLICT, EXPECTED_PARENT_MISMATCH, PREFLIGHT_PASS_OVERCLAIMS_WORK_LANDED) before treating any change as landed.
- **First command:** `microcosm mission-transaction-work-spine run --input fixtures/first_wave/mission_transaction_work_spine/input --out receipts/first_wave/mission_transaction_work_spine`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates work-landing, claim, checkpoint-lane, and dependency metadata projections over fixed fixtures only; it does not mutate live ledgers or git, certify real closeout, authorize broad staging without operator intent, or prove any change is actually correct or complete.
- **Wiring:** wired — consumes the shared work_landing, mission_transaction_preflight, and Work Ledger macro-tool source imports plus the shared secret-exclusion/receipts/schemas spine; it supplies the source-import anchor surface that concurrency_mission_control uses for Work Ledger seed-speed topology instead of duplicating Work Ledger bodies. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
- **Source relations:** `100` source-module edges · `12` source files · `77` source shards · `12` target files · `77` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=mission_transaction_work_spine]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ mission_transaction_work_spine`
- **Wires to:** `bounded_autonomy_campaign_packet`, `concurrency_mission_control`, `macro_projection_import_protocol`, `tool_server_pressure_inventory`, `batch10_live_source_drift_capsule`, `batch4_proof_authority_runtime`, `batch8_tools_tail_primitives_capsule`, `agent_benchmark_integrity_anti_gaming_replay`, `agentic_vulnerability_discovery_patch_proof_replay`

### Durable Agent Work Landing Replay

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Work, Landing & Continuity

- **Organ id:** `durable_agent_work_landing_replay`
- **Specialty:** `workflow-engineering` · `continuity`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `4` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** This checks recorded examples of an agent finishing a piece of work the careful way: each example must name the exact files it claims to have touched, show that validation was recorded before any commit was attempted, and only label itself "committed" if the example also records the repository's HEAD moving. It also checks that blockers and ledger closeout were captured. The check shows whether each recorded work-claim carries the required evidence and ordering, rather than being an unbacked chat boast. It judges the recorded claims against the contract; it does not run Git or prove that any commit truly landed in a real repository.
- **For agents:** An agent runs this over recorded work-landing rows to confirm they satisfy the declared contract (each row cites owned claimed paths and owner-native validation refs; validation precedes the commit attempt; a "committed-landed" claim carries a declared HEAD-before != HEAD-after advance; metadata-blocked rows capture a blocker ref; Work Ledger closeout is cited) and to confirm the fixture's nine negative rows are each rejected with their expected error codes. It validates the recorded contract only; it does not execute Git or prove real-world landedness.
- **First command:** `microcosm durable-agent-work-landing-replay run-work-landing-bundle`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public work-landing contract over recorded rows; it does not mutate Git, stage unrelated dirty paths, prove a commit actually landed, export private source bodies, call providers, publish/host, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret_exclusion_scan, receipts, schemas). Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

### Bridge Phase Continuity Runtime

`semantic_validator` · strength 5/5 · Work, Landing & Continuity

- **Organ id:** `bridge_phase_continuity_runtime`
- **Specialty:** `workflow-engineering` · `continuity`
- **Substrate disposition:** `real_substrate_capsule` · real bodies `5` · receipt bodies `0` · fixture role `regression_wrapper_around_real_substrate`
- **What it makes visible:** Replays a small synthetic record of a paused background job to check that the rules for safely resuming it hold. It confirms the job left behind a real resume note, that trying to resume the same job twice is refused, that "still alive" pings can never count as permission to resume (and a long-stale ping can't be passed off as proof the job is still healthy), and that only a proper close-out receipt is allowed to claim the work actually finished. It also includes deliberately broken cases to prove each rule rejects them. These continuity rules are inspectable in plain receipts without any live job, network call, or private data ever being touched.
- **For agents:** An agent runs it to confirm, against a public synthetic fixture, that detached-job continuity rules hold before relying on them: a resumable job needs a continuation packet, a packet resumes exactly once (duplicate resume is rejected), heartbeats are liveness-only (and stale heartbeats are not live-health evidence), resource pressure blocks dispatch as a recorded decision, worker-skip rows dedupe without silently closing a claim, and only a closeout transition receipt marks work as landed. Each rule is exercised by a matching negative case.
- **First command:** `microcosm bridge-phase-continuity-runtime run --input fixtures/second_wave/bridge_phase_continuity_runtime/input --out /tmp/microcosm-bridge-continuity`
- **Does not authorize:** It validates only the declared public continuity contract over synthetic fixtures; it does not run live bridge transport, call providers, read operator HUD/browser/phase-runtime or private-memory state, prove provider or UI uptime, land work, mutate source, or authorize release.
- **Wiring:** standalone_specimen — binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (receipts, private-state scan, strict-JSON schemas). Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
- **Source relations:** `35` source-module edges · `5` source files · `30` source shards · `5` target files · `30` target shards · handle `organ-surface-contract::coverage.source_module_file_graph.edges_by_organ[organ_id=bridge_phase_continuity_runtime]` · drilldown `microcosm organ-topology --organ bridge_phase_continuity_runtime`
